name: Diamond Head Summit
tagline: Oahu's most iconic crater climb — tunnels, stairs, and 360-degree views that justify the hype.
bestTime: Sunrise — book the 6am slot weeks in advance; it sells out
kamaaina: Hawaii residents free entry with valid state ID; $5 parking (vs $10 non-resident)
talkStory: Hawaiians called it Le'ahi ('brow of the ahi fish') for its profile — British sailors in 1825 mistook calcite crystals for diamonds and the English name stuck. The US Army built Fort Ruger here in 1908, carving the tunnels and fire control station you climb through today as a coastal defense post to spot enemy ships. The spiral staircase and dark tunnel were part of the military observation network. Book reservations at gostateparks.hawaii.gov — the 6am sunrise slot sells out weeks ahead.
